perturbation.original_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.changed_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; ARIN shall be liable for disclosure of such information in all circumstances. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes all exceptions to ARIN's liability for disclosure. This directly contradicts the original clause that lists specific instances where ARIN would *not* be liable, now ARIN is liable in all circumstances. This creates a direct conflict within Paragraph 4, because initially ARIN's liability was specifically restricted, now it is absolute. This contradicts the earlier part of section (4), in which ARIN takes reasonable care to prevent disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; ARIN shall be liable for disclosure of such information in all circumstances. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exceptions to ARIN's liability for disclosure of proprietary information, stating that ARIN 'shall be liable for disclosure of such information in all circumstances.' This directly contradicts the original intent of paragraph 4, which, as mentioned in the explanation, had exceptions where ARIN would *not* be liable. Since the contradicted text is a modified version of the original clause (4), a direct contradiction is created within the same section where ARIN initially takes 'reasonable care' but is now liable in all circumstances, violating the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: (5) The standa rd of care for protecting Proprietary Information imposed on ARIN wi ll be th at degree of care ARIN uses to prevent disclosure, publication or dissemination of its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (5) The standard of care for protecting Proprietary Information imposed on ARIN will be no care at all. ARIN is not responsible for protecting Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly negates ARIN's responsibility for protecting Proprietary Information. This contradicts the purpose of the entire Non-Disclosure Agreement and directly conflicts with section (4), where ARIN agrees to take 'reasonable care'. If ARIN takes no care at all, it undermines the entire agreement's intention of protecting applicant's data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that ARIN will take 'no care at all' and is 'not responsible' for protecting Proprietary Information. This directly contradicts the original text, which states ARIN 'will take reasonable care' to prevent disclosure in section (4). This is a strong and direct conflict of obligation, satisfying the condition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction and directly contradicts the provided text.

perturbation.original_text: (1) "Proprietary Information" shall be defined as and limited to, (a) network engineering plans, including subnets, and host counts, and hosts per subnet with projected utilization rates and associated confidence levels of those projections for one and two years in the future; (b) deployment schedules for the network, including major milestones for each subnet; (c) network topology diagrams originated by the Applicant, not previously published or otherwise disclosed to the general public, not previously available without restriction to ARIN or others, and not normally furnished to others without compensation; and (d) such other information which the Applicant desires to protect against unrestricted disclosure or competitive use, and which the parties mutually agree shall be furnished pursuant to this Non-Disclosure Agreement and which is app ropriately identified as being proprietary when initially furnished to ARIN.
perturbation.changed_text: (1) "Proprietary Information" shall be defined as any information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN, regardless of whether it meets the criteria outlined in (a) through (d) below. (a) network engineering plans, including subnets, and host counts, and hosts per subnet with projected utilization rates and associated confidence levels of those projections for one and two years in the future; (b) deployment schedules for the network, including major milestones for each subnet; (c) network topology diagrams originated by the Applicant, not previously published or otherwise disclosed to the general public, not previously available without restriction to ARIN or others, and not normally furnished to others without compensation; and (d) such other information which the Applicant desires to protect against unrestricted disclosure or competitive use, and which the parties mutually agree shall be furnished pursuant to this Non-Disclosure Agreement and which is app ropriately identified as being proprietary when initially furnished to ARIN.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Proprietary Information' to include *any* information disclosed, regardless of whether it meets the following listed criteria. This creates a contradiction because the original definition limits what qualifies as proprietary information, now all information is proprietary information. This undermines the specific requirements outlined in (a)-(d).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; (b) clearly identified as Proprietary Information at the time of its disclosure by each page thereof being marked with an appropriate legend, in bold faced print, indicating that the information is deemed proprietary by the Applicant; and (c) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Proprietary Information' to encompass any information disclosed, regardless of the original criteria (a)-(d). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that 'Proprietary Information' must be (a) in writing, (b) clearly identified as Proprietary Information, and (c) delivered via specific methods. The changed definition renders these requirements meaningless because all disclosed information is now automatically considered proprietary, regardless of whether it meets these conditions. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the specific criteria for protection outlined in the contradicted text are undermined by the new all-encompassing definition.

perturbation.original_text: (6) This Non-Disclosure Agreement contains the entire agreement relative to the protection of information to be provided to AR IN by the Applicant, and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written understandings or arguments regarding this issue. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not be modified or amended, except in a written instrument executed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) This Non-Disclosure Agreement contains the entire agreement relative to the protection of information to be provided to AR IN by the Applicant, and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written understandings or arguments regarding this issue. This Non-Disclosure Agreement can be modified orally by either party at any time, and such modifications will be binding even without written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the NDA can *only* be modified in writing. This change now permits *oral* modifications, directly contradicting the prior sentence. This creates confusion as to the enforceability of any changes to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) This Non-Disclosure Agreement contains the entire agreement relative to the protection of information to be provided to AR IN by the Applicant, and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written understandings or arguments regarding this issue. This Non-Disclosure Agreement can be modified orally by either party at any time, and such modifications will be binding even without written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the NDA can only be modified in writing. The changed text explicitly allows for oral modifications, which directly contradicts the initial requirement for written modifications. This conflict regarding how the NDA can be modified creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means that the contradiction is self-contained within the modified section itself.

perturbation.original_text: (3) In order for the Applicant's Proprietary Information to be protected as described herein, the Proprietary Information must be delivered in written form as discussed in Paragraph 2 above to: American Registry for Internet Numbers Attn: Business Office PO Box 232290 Centreville, VA 20120 USA
perturbation.changed_text: (3) In order for the Applicant's Proprietary Information to be protected as described herein, the Proprietary Information can be delivered in written or verbal form, to any ARIN employee, or to anyone on the street without it affecting the non disclosure agreement: American Registry for Internet Numbers Attn: Business Office PO Box 232290 Centreville, VA 20120 USA
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original agreement requiring proprietary information to be submitted in written form. By allowing verbal submissions, it weakens the protection of sensitive data and challenges the formal submission process. The addition of submitting proprietary information to 'anyone on the street' directly opposes the intended confidential nature of the agreement and makes the agreement innefective. It goes against the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; (b) clearly identified as Proprietary Information at the time of its disclosure by each page thereof being marked with an appropriate legend, in bold faced print, indicating that the information is deemed proprietary by the Applicant; and (c) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text require that the Proprietary Information must be delivered in written form. The changed text creates a direct contradiction by stating that the Proprietary Information can be delivered in written or verbal form, or even to 'anyone on the street' and it won't affect the non disclosure agreement, which directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states in (2)(a) that information 'must be in writing'. The explanation confirms this by stating 'By allowing verbal submissions, it weakens the protection of sensitive data and challenges the formal submission process. The addition of submitting proprietary information to 'anyone on the street' directly opposes the intended confidential nature of the agreement and makes the agreement ineffective.'

perturbation.original_text: (9) This Non-Disclosure Agreement may not be assigned or otherwise transferred by either party in wh ole or in part without the express prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not unreasonably be withheld. This consent requirement shall not apply in the event either party shall change its corporate name or merge with another corporation. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall benefit and be binding upon the successors and assigns of the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (9) This Non-Disclosure Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred by either party in whole or in part without any consent needed. This consent requirement shall not apply in the event either party shall change its corporate name or merge with another corporation. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not benefit or be binding upon the successors and assigns of the parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The change eliminates the requirement for written consent to assign or transfer the NDA. This contradicts the original clause, which explicitly required written consent. Now, assignment can occur freely. It also now states that the agreement will not benefit/bind successors, directly opposing the original intent. This change undermines the stability and enforceability of the NDA, particularly in scenarios involving mergers or acquisitions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (9) This Non-Disclosure Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred by either party in whole or in part without any consent needed. This consent requirement shall not apply in the event either party shall change its corporate name or merge with another corporation. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not benefit or be binding upon the successors and assigns of the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section (9) eliminates the requirement for written consent for assignment or transfer, directly contradicting the original clause that mandated such consent. Additionally, it now states that the agreement will not bind successors, opposing the original intent. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are the same in this case, it is a clear and direct example of structural flaws and creates an in-text contradiction.
